sourceMovie.title: The Wrestler
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit The Wrestler for the same reason it broke through in 2008: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a romance rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Darren Aronofsky keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The drama and romance blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: First Daughter
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Wrestler clicked for you, First Daughter (2004) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and romance, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Wrestler?
faq.answer: Try First Daughter, Milk Money, I, Tonya—each shares DNA with The Wrestler while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Wrestler?
faq.answer: The Wrestler (2008) is best described as Drama, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Wrestler worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
